_comment: Example .claude-plugin/marketplace-metadata.json — copy to your marketplace repo and adjust. See docs/curated-marketplace-format.md for the full schema. The `_comment` keys are ignored by Agnes — they're here purely as inline documentation; remove them in production.

data-explorer._comment: Plugin-level enrichment — surfaces on /marketplace cards and on the plugin detail page.
data-explorer.tagline: Catalogue lookups + schema drill-down for the data warehouse — no SQL required.
data-explorer.description:
**Data Explorer** answers everyday warehouse questions without you writing SQL:

- Find a table by topic, owner, or freshness.
- Drill into a table's schema, sample rows, lineage upstream / downstream.
- Cross-reference dashboards that already query the table.

Powered by the daily catalogue sync that Agnes ingests into DuckDB — every read is local and millisecond-fast. No BigQuery cost, no risk of touching production.

use_cases.title: Find the right table
use_cases.description: When you know what data you need but not where it lives.
use_cases.prompt: Where can I find daily revenue by merchant?

use_cases.title: Schema drill-down
use_cases.description: Inspect columns, types, nullability, and sample values for one table.

use_cases.title: Lineage upstream / downstream
use_cases.description: See which jobs write a table and which dashboards / models depend on it.

sample_interaction.user: Where can I find daily revenue by merchant for the last 30 days?
sample_interaction.assistant:
The closest match is **`analytics.merchant_revenue_daily`** (last updated 4 hours ago).

- **Primary key**: `(merchant_id, revenue_date)`
- **Granularity**: daily
- **Owner**: Analytics Platform team — `analytics-platform@example.com`
- **Source**: aggregated from `orders.fact_revenue` via the `merchant_revenue_rollup` dbt model

For live (intraday) revenue, see `analytics.merchant_revenue_hourly` instead — it's refreshed every 15 min but only retains the last 7 days.

explore-table._comment: Skill-level enrichment — visual fields only for now. Rich content fields (display_name / tagline / description / use_cases / sample_interaction) are accepted by the schema but not yet rendered for skills/agents in the UI; will activate in a later phase.

report-generator._comment: Minimal example — only a cover photo + category override. Other fields default to absent and the UI hides those sections.
